elements.sent-2411-005: Existing feed-forward image-to-3D methods mainly rely on 2D multi-view diffusion models that cannot guarantee 3D consistency.
elements.sent-2411-006: These methods easily collapse when changing the prompt view direction and mainly handle object-centric prompt images.
elements.sent-2411-007: In this paper, we propose a novel single-stage 3D diffusion model, DiffusionGS, for object and scene generation from a single view.
elements.sent-2411-008: DiffusionGS directly outputs 3D Gaussian point clouds at each timestep to enforce view consistency and allow the model to generate robustly given prompt views of any directions, beyond object-centric inputs.
elements.sent-2411-009: Plus, to improve the capability and generalization ability of DiffusionGS, we scale up 3D training data by developing a scene-object mixed training strategy.
elements.sent-2411-010: Experiments show that our method enjoys better generation quality (2.20 dB $\uparrow$ PSNR and 23.25 $\downarrow$ FID) and over 5$\times$ faster speed ($\sim$6s on an A100 GPU) than SOTA methods.. Project page: \url{https://caiyuanhao1998.github.io/project/DiffusionGS/.}

elements.sent-2411-011: Image-to-3D generation is an important and challenging task that aims to generate a 3D representation of scenes or objects given only a single-view image.
elements.sent-2411-012: It has wide applications in AR/VR~\cite{ar_1 , film making~\cite{film_1 , robotics~\cite{robotics_1,robotics_2 , animation~\cite{animation_1,animation_2 , gaming~\cite{gaming_1 , and so on.}}}}}
elements.sent-2411-013: Existing feed-forward image-to-3D methods are mainly two-stage~\cite{lgm,12345++,crm,instant3d .}
elements.sent-2411-014: They firstly adopt a 2D diffusion model to generate blocked multi-view images and secondly feed the multi-view images into a 3D reconstruction model.
elements.sent-2411-015: Without 3D model in the diffusion, these methods cannot enforce view consistency and easily collapse when the prompt view direction changes.
elements.sent-2411-016: Another less studied technical route~\cite{renderdiffusion,viewsetdiff,dmv3d is to train a 3D diffusion model with 2D rendering loss.}
elements.sent-2411-017: Yet, these methods mainly rely on triplane neural radiance field (NeRF)~\cite{nerf .}
elements.sent-2411-018: The volume rendering of NeRF is time-consuming and the triplane resolution is limited, preventing the model from scaling up to larger scenes.
elements.sent-2411-019: In addition, current methods mainly study object-level generation using only object-centric datasets to train, while limits the model generalization ability and leaves larger-scale scene-level generation less explored.
elements.sent-2411-020: To address these issues, we propose a novel single-stage 3D Gaussian Splatting (3DGS)~\cite{3dgs based diffusion model, DiffusionGS, for 3D object and scene generation from a single view.}
elements.sent-2411-021: Our DiffusionGS enforces 3D consistency of the generated contents by predicting multi-view pixel-aligned Gaussian primitives in every timestep.
elements.sent-2411-022: With the highly parallel rasterization and scalable imaging range, DiffusionGS enjoys a fast inference speed of $\sim$6 seconds per asset and can be easily applied to large scenes.
elements.sent-2411-023: Since our goal is to build a general and large-scale 3D generation model, it is critical to fully exploit existing 3D scene and object data.
elements.sent-2411-024: However, directly training with scene and object data may lead to non-convergence because of the large domain discrepancy.
elements.sent-2411-025: Thus, we propose a scene-object mixed training strategy to handle this problem and learn a general prior of geometry and texture.
elements.sent-2411-026: Our mixed training strategy adapts DiffusionGS to both object and scene datasets by controlling the distribution of the selected views, camera condition, Gaussian point clouds, and imaging depths.
elements.sent-2411-027: In particular, we notice previous camera conditioning method Pl\"ucker coordinate~\cite{plucker_coordinate shows limitations in capturing depth and 3D geometry.}
elements.sent-2411-028: Hence, we design a new camera conditioning method, Reference-Point Pl\"ucker Coordinates (RPPC), that encodes the point on each ray closest to the origin of the world coordinate system to help DiffusionGS better perceive the depth and 3D geometry across scene and object data.
elements.sent-2411-029: Finally, the mixed training weights are further finetuned on object or scene data to boost the performance.

elements.sent-2411-030: Our contributions can be summarized as follows:.
elements.sent-2411-031: We propose a novel 3DGS-based diffusion model, DiffusionGS, for single-view 3D object and scene generation.
elements.sent-2411-032: We design a scene-object mixed training strategy to learn a more general prior from both 3D object and scene data.
elements.sent-2411-033: We customize a new camera pose conditioning method, RPPC, to better perceive the relative depth and geometry.
elements.sent-2411-034: DiffusionGS yields more visually pleasant results and outperforms previous methods by 2.20 dB/23.25 and 2.91 dB/75.68 in PSNR/FID on object- and scene-level generation with a fast speed of $\sim$6s on a single A100 GPU.
elements.sent-2411-035: Diffusion models~\cite{ddpm,score_dm,ddim,stable_diffusion,sdedit are proposed for image generation and recently have been applied to 3D generation, which can be divided into four categories.}
elements.sent-2411-036: The first category~\cite{point-e, shape-e, direct_3d_1, direct_3d_2, direct_3d_3, diffgs, gaussiancube uses direct supervision on 3D models such as point clouds or meshes, which are hard to obtain in practice.}
elements.sent-2411-037: The second kind of methods~\cite{dreamgaussian,gaussiandreamer,hugs,luciddreamer,physdreamer,prolificdreamer,dreamreward,dreambooth3d use SDS loss~\cite{sds to distill a 3D model from a 2D diffusion.}}
elements.sent-2411-038: Yet, these methods require a time-consuming per-asset optimization.
elements.sent-2411-039: The third category~\cite{zero123, view_diff_1, shi2023mvdream, gu2023nerfdiff, liu2023syncdreamer, wonder3d adds the camera poses as the input condition to finetune a 2D diffusion model to render fixed novel views.}
elements.sent-2411-040: Yet, these methods cannot guarantee 3D consistency and easily collapse when prompt view direction changes.
elements.sent-2411-041: The last category~\cite{dmv3d,renderdiffusion,viewsetdiff trains a 3D diffusion model with 2D rendering loss.}
elements.sent-2411-042: However, these methods mainly based on triplane-NeRF suffer from the limited resolution of triplane and slow speed of volume rendering.
elements.sent-2411-043: It achieves success in 3D/4D reconstruction~\cite{mip-gs, x_gaussian, FSGS, dynamic1, dynamic2, dynamic3, lightgaussian, lsm, cogs, mvsplat, geogaussian , generation~\cite{gaussiandreamer,luciddreamer,humangaussian,hugs,gauhuman,gaussiancube,diffgs,lucidfusion, gaussianeditor, gsd , inverse rendering~\cite{InverseRendering1,InverseRendering2,InverseRendering3 , SLAM~\cite{slam1,slam2,slam3, slam4 , etc. For instance, GS-LRM~\cite{gslrm develops a regression model to reconstruct a 3DGS from four views with camera poses.}}}}}

elements.sent-2411-091: To improve the capacity and generalization ability of our DiffusionGS, it is critical to make full use of both object and scene data.
